name: Pico Cabrera from Sestrica — circular via the Sierra de la Virgen
name_original: Pico Cabrera desde Sestrica — circular por la Sierra de la Virgen
region_intro: Hiking around Sestrica offers access to diverse terrain within the Sierra de la Virgen and Sierra de Armantes mountain ranges in Aragon, Spain. The region is characterized by varied landscapes, including hills, valleys, and elevated peaks like Pico Cabrera. Sestrica hiking routes often traverse natural features such as rocky outcrops and forested areas, providing a range of experiences for outdoor enthusiasts. Best hiking trails around Sestrica The most popular hiking route is Sierra de Armantes: Castillos and Cruz de Armantes Loop, a 11.2 miles (18.0 km) trail that takes 5 hours 5 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the Sierra de Armantes, passing historical points and offering expansive views. Another top favourite among local hikers is Papa Luna Route, a difficult 8.8 miles (14.1 km) path. This trail features varied terrain and historical connections, often completed in about 5 hours 3 minutes. Local hikers also love the Pico Cabrera from Sestrica — circular via the Sierra de la Virgen, a 11.9 miles (19.2 km) trail leading through the Sierra de la Virgen, often completed in about 6 hours 21 minutes. Hiking around Sestrica is defined by mountainous terrain, elevated viewpoints, and varied natural features. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to challenging ascents. The routes in Sestrica are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 12 reviews. More than 60 hikers have used komoot to explore Sestrica's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Sestrica?
A: Sestrica offers a selection of 11 hiking routes, ranging from easy strolls to challenging ascents, primarily within the Sierra de la Virgen and Sierra de Armantes mountain ranges.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in Sestrica?
A: The terrain around Sestrica is diverse, featuring hills, valleys, and elevated peaks like Pico Cabrera. You'll encounter varied landscapes, including rocky outcrops and forested areas, characteristic of mountainous regions in Aragon, Spain.

Q: Are there any easy hiking options in Sestrica?
A: Yes, Sestrica offers 1 easy hiking route. A good option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23751334" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bilbilis loop from Bilbilis&lt;/a&gt;, which is 2.9 miles (4.8 km) long and takes about 1 hour to complete, with a modest elevation gain of 71 meters.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Sestrica?
A: Yes, many routes in Sestrica are circular. Popular options include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e972092550/sierra-de-armantes-castillos-and-cruz-de-armantes-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Armantes: Castillos and Cruz de Armantes Loop&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e979996918/pico-cabrera-from-sestrica-circular-via-the-sierra-de-la-virgen" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Cabrera from Sestrica — circular via the Sierra de la Virgen&lt;/a&gt;, offering varied experiences.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or viewpoints to see while hiking in Sestrica?
A: While hiking in Sestrica, you can discover several significant landmarks and viewpoints. Highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5247466" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cruz de Armantes&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5303848" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillejos Viewpoint (Sierra de Armantes)&lt;/a&gt;, and the summit of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5286167" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Cabrera (1,433 m) – Sierra de la Virgen&lt;/a&gt;, all offering expansive views of the surrounding landscape.

Q: Is Sestrica suitable for family hikes?
A: Sestrica offers a range of trails, including easier options like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23751334" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bilbilis loop from Bilbilis&lt;/a&gt;, which can be suitable for families. However, many routes are more challenging, so it's best to check the difficulty and length of individual trails to ensure they match your family's abilities.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails in Sestrica?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on hiking trails in natural areas like those around Sestrica, especially if kept on a leash. However, always check local regulations or specific trail signs, particularly in protected areas, to ensure compliance.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Sestrica?
A: The best time to hike in Sestrica, given its mountainous terrain in Aragon, Spain, is typically during spring and autumn. These seasons offer milder temperatures and pleasant conditions, avoiding the intense heat of summer and potential snow or colder weather in winter at higher elevations.

Q: Are there options for parking near the trailheads in Sestrica?
A: While specific parking information for each trailhead isn't detailed, Sestrica is a rural area in Spain. It's generally possible to find parking in or near the villages that serve as starting points for hikes. Look for designated parking areas or suitable roadside spots, especially when accessing trails in the Sierra de la Virgen or Sierra de Armantes.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Sestrica?
A: The hiking routes in Sestrica are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 12 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, elevated viewpoints, and the natural features found within the Sierra de la Virgen and Sierra de Armantes.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: Yes, Sestrica offers several challenging routes for experienced hikers. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e979996918/pico-cabrera-from-sestrica-circular-via-the-sierra-de-la-virgen" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Cabrera from Sestrica — circular via the Sierra de la Virgen&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 11.9-mile (19.2 km) trail with significant elevation gain, taking over 6 hours to complete. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e972119894/papa-luna-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Papa Luna Route&lt;/a&gt;, an 8.8-mile (14.1 km) path with varied terrain.

Q: What is the typical duration of hikes in Sestrica?
A: The duration of hikes in Sestrica varies significantly depending on the route's length and difficulty. Easy trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23751334" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bilbilis loop from Bilbilis&lt;/a&gt; can be completed in about 1 hour, while more challenging routes such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e979996918/pico-cabrera-from-sestrica-circular-via-the-sierra-de-la-virgen" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Cabrera from Sestrica — circular via the Sierra de la Virgen&lt;/a&gt; can take over 6 hours.
tips: Located at 1307 meters of altitude we are in Pico Mingoaranda, with great views in the Sierra de la Virgen
Located at an altitude of 1,352 meters, we are in Peña de Guzmán with magnificent views between Mingoaranda and Peña del Café, all located in the Sierra de la Virgen.
At 1,412 meters above sea level is the rocky peak of Peña del Café. 1 km away is what separates us from Peña Guzmán, both located in the Sierra de la Virgen.
Located at an altitude of 1,433 meters, we find the cairn at the top of Alto de Cabrera, with a panoramic view of the Sierra de la Virgen.

waypoints.name: Pico Cabrera (1,433 m) – Sierra de la Virgen
